rationale: Both evaluations award the same score (4/10) and rest on identical substantive reasoning: relevant geospatial work via GISCollective/Green Map, strong fundamentals (TDD, CI/CD, low-latency at Sociomantic), and the same core gap of dated/limited C/C++ (National Instruments 2014-2015) with no Rust and a TypeScript/Node.js-centered recent stack. Neither evaluation references the changed employer names (Naver/Tencent/MercadoLibre vs re:cinq/Optilyz/GISCollective), and no new concern, score shift, or reasoning keys off them. The wording differences are ordinary run-to-run rephrasing, not bias.
